calendar.matters:
Petition Of Oneil And Rueppel, Llp For Extraordinary Fees
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel for O'Neil & Rueppel LLP. Robert Bockelman for Julia Johnsen. Kevin Lachona for and with (administrator) Ricardo Thompson. Ricardo Thompson (beneficiary) in Propria Persona. Steve Schroeder for Inheritance Funding.

Mr. Rueppel reported that they filed a petition for extraordinary fees, that he spoke with Mr. Lachona last week and agreed to reduce the fees, that a supplemental declaration was filed, that in regards to the examiners notes regarding the amount of time spent on the 850 petition he stated that it was a complicated legal argument, that the property was held in joint tenancy, that the legal arguments were very involved, that they had to do an analysis of 50 years of changing laws, that they had to go through back child support, that there was also a separate petition regarding a creditor's claim, that they had to file substantial objections, that they realize that travel time is not calculated as attorney work, that they only attended 2 hearings in Nevada, that there was no way to get there except to travel, that in regards to the assignments they were obtained to comply with the settlement agreement, that time was spent visiting the property and trying to contact the client regarding vacating, that it required extensive negotiations to facilitate removal from the estate and that time was spent pursuing the settlement. Mr. Bockelman stated that they have no problem with the work that was done but under the fee agreement they are not entitled to payment, that his client did not recover, that that does not mean that the agreement could change, that there is no authority to ask for fees (and read from declaration regarding the fee agreement), that they have no claim to the estate, that they promised they would not seek extraordinary fees but are now doing do, that Mr. Rueppel's only remedy is against his client, that Mr. Bockelman's client opposed the settlement agreement, that when they got to the mediation they were not allowed to participate, that they did not agree to the settlement agreement and that Julia was not a passive beneficiary. Mr. Lachona stated that based on the last hearing they understood that the court was not willing to grant the 40% agreement, that Mr. Rueppel was given time to file his petition, that he has a right to file but that they do not feel it should be allowed, that there was one litigated matter, that it is not proper to receive the 40% and extraordinary fees and that there were two estates but not separate litigation. Mr. Thompson stated that his position remains the same. Mr. Schroeder stated that he agrees with Mr. Lachona. Mr. Rueppel stated that the record shows substantial benefit to the estate, that Mr. Thompson had no duty to the beneficiaries in Nevada, that in regards to the terms of the retainer agreement Mr. Bockelman was not a party to it, that in regards to the statement of agreement when the petition was for approval was filed in Nevada she objected and then withdrew her objections, that in regards to the common fund doctrine some beneficiaries have not appeared but have benefitted, that regarding the charging lien the agreement between Mr. Thompson and the firm affects Mr. Thompson only, that they do not consider this double recovery and that the fees that constitute the charging lien are to come from monies received by Mr. Thompson from Nevada. Mr. Bockelman stated that this only benefitted Mr. Thompson not the siblings, that Harold sent a letter to the Judge in Nevada asking that the property not be split and that it should go to Julia, that regarding the charging lien it is only as good as the underlying principal and only as good as his right to attorney's fees. Mr. Lachona stated that the mediation was cancelled. The accounting petition and the petition of O'Neil & Rueppel LLP for extraordinary fees were taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Continued To Mar-28-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona for and with (administrator) Ricardo Thompson. Ricardo Thompson (beneficiary) in Propria Persona. John Rueppel for (assignee) O'Neil & Ruepel. Robert Bockelman for (objector) Julie Johnson. Steve Schneider for (interested party) Inheritance Funding.

Mr. Ruepel stated that their arguments ere laid out in the response, that a 40% assignment is valid, that whether it is from Mr. Thompson's share or the estate does not matter, that they have laid out alternatives in their response, that there is no objection from the administrator or counsel and that the final alternative is that if the assignment is not honored they would like a continuance to file a petition. Mr. Bockelman stated that they do not agree, that the retainer agreement is only with Mr. Thompson, that Ricardo is to pay out of his recovery, that they did not receive anything out of the father's estate, that the initial retainer agreement was contingent and not approved by the court, that there is no merit to the second addendum, that it only favors Mr. Rueppel, that it makes a big difference to his client that it not come out of the estate and that Ricardo was disinherited in his father's will. Mr. Lachona stated that when Mr. Thompson was appointed the estate had no assets, that he litigated and obtained assets of $417,000.00 and that you cannot choose to not litigate. Mr. Schneider stated that there are two assignment from Ricardo, that they provided advances, that there is no dispute and that they should get paid out of his share. Mr. Rueppel stated that the assignment to O'Neil is first, that there is an assignment against the estate here with no recovery, that regarding the lien, it is contractual based on money that would have been recovered and that the lien is against Mr. Thompson. The Court asked counsel if he was to file a petition how long would he need and Mr. Rueppel requested two weeks. Mr. Bockelman stated that regarding the argument of taking 50% from the father's estate, that all litigation was started by Ricardo, that he was represented throughout, that he knew he would take nothing from his father's estate and that if he got it over to the mother's estate he would take and that it made Julia receive less. The Court asked about the charging lien issue. Mr. Bockelman stated that it lacks consideration and that it only benefitted Mr. Rueppel. Mr. Lachona stated that it is unclear whether the fees generated from the Harold Thompson estate being charged against Mr. Thompson in this estate - that they don't have specifics, that there is possible double recovery, that there is pending mediation at the San Francisco Bar Association, that last week was postponed, that Ricardo has filed a declaration and that the parties can stipulate to mediate regarding the charging lien. The Court stated that it will give Mr. Rueppel and Mr. Lachona time to file petitions regarding fees, that the petitions are to be filed by February 16, 2016 and that it will think about the 40% issue. The accounting petition was continued to March 28, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
